title: We should tell each other what to think

transcript.text: Hello, this video is we should all be telling each other what to think and what to do.
It's our job as human beings in the civilized world, and it's something that we really need to start realizing now, and we don't realize it enough.
The time has come for us to change our personalities and morph into the kinds of people who tell each other what to think.
The motivation behind this video is on my Where Buddhism Went Wrong, which is my most highly commented video by far.
I get a comment on it every other day, and by far my lowest rated video at least three times and probably even more than that.
I've gotten people, first they're like, oh, screw you, or whatever, and then I'm like, no, but I'm just trying to tell you what I think.
Anyways, three times I've heard people go, well, you shouldn't tell anybody else what to think, and yes, we should be telling each other what to think.
It is our job. Not only is it something that's okay to do, it's something that we should all do.
I think maybe one reason some people have an aversion to that is because they don't have anything to say.
They don't have any ideas, and they don't have anything to tell other people what to think,
And if they can't do it, nobody can type of an idea.
You know, like they get jealous because they're not smart enough to, original enough to come up with any ideas,
So then if they can't do it, then fuck you, you can't do it too.
Maybe that's a motivation for it.
But that's the way of the old world.
And it's a way to break out of the old world.
The old world is we all keep our mouths shut, we don't have any opinions, and we let the other dude tell us what to think.
The Catholic Church, for example, telling everybody what to think and what to do until, you know, they basically weren't people.
I mean, I think it's a holdover, actually, from that mentality.
People are just still stuck in that mentality.
The Catholic Church fought against the propagation of the printing press.
They didn't want the printing press to come about because they didn't want people reading the Bibles.
I saw a documentary a couple weeks ago where some woman became a Muslim.
She was a Catholic nun because she said Catholic nuns aren't allowed to read the Bible.
And it's a great way to get out of the centralization.

Three times on that video, and then another time I was like, yeah, man, I'm just, you know, I can't remember how it started, but he's like, what?
You shouldn't tell anybody what to think.
People should be able to think for themselves.
And us all telling each other what to think is the way that we get to think for ourselves.
Because I'm not, I mean, you don't get it confused.
I'm not trying to say that we should make laws so that whoever's in first place gets to tell everybody what's to think.
And if they don't think what he thinks, then they all go to jail or they get fined or something.
No, I'm saying we should all tell each other what to think.
I'm not saying we should all be forced to do what we all tell each other to do.
We should just all be telling each other what to think.
Because what it does is it creates a level playing field and it expands the talent pool.
